data.title: U.S. Futures Fall, European Stocks Rise After Trump's Tariff Pause
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.5% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 0.3%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 6.9% in morning trading. Computacenter rose 23% and Barclays added 17.7%. On the other hand, Barry Callebaut dropped 15.2%, and Saipem sunk 7.4%. The FTSE 100 added 5.5%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 6.8% and Germany's DAX climbed 7.3%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index fell 0.2% to 98.9.

In commodities, Brent crude rose 2.8% to $64.58 a barrel, and WTI crude fell 1.3% to $61.57 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 5.1% to 35.38 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 11 basis points to 2.695% from 2.59%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury declined by 3 basis points to 4.329% from 4.356%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were up as Japan's Nikkei 225 index climbed 9.1%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 2.5%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 1.2%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: RCS - LifeSafe Holdings - Netherlands and Benelux Distribution Agreement
data.description:
RNS Number : 4882E LifeSafe Holdings PLC 10 April 2025  

10 April 2025                                                                                                       

This is a Reach (i.e. non-regulatory) announcement and the information contained is not considered to be material or to have a significant impact on management's expectations of the Company's performance.

LifeSafe Holdings plc

("LifeSafe", the "Group" or the "Company")

Netherlands and Benelux Distribution Agreement

LifeSafe (AIM: LIFS), a fire safety technology business with innovative fire extinguishing and prevention fluids and fire safety products, is pleased to announce the signing of a distribution agreement between LifeSafe and Flame Control B.V. ("Flame Control") for the supply of the Company's range of fire extinguishers and bulk fluids (the "Agreement").

Under the terms of the Agreement, LifeSafe will supply Flame Control with the Company's fire extinguishers filled with the LifeSafe's Multi-Purpose Fluid ("MPF") and Thermal Runaway Fluid ("TRF") on an exclusive basis in the Netherlands and Benelux.  The Board expects this Agreement to generate sales in the region of €800,000 over the next two years. The Agreement is complementary to, and permitted under, the global distribution agreement announced on 26 March 2025 with subsidiaries of IDEX Fire & Safety.

Flame Control

Founded in 2012, Flame Control is a fast-growing fire protection service and maintenance company. Since 1 March 2024, Flame Control has been part-owned by the Premier Technical Services Group ("PTSG"), which employs 2,700 people across 48 sites throughout the UK and Europe, supporting over 20,000 customers and maintaining more than 200,000 properties.

Rodny Bosma, Managing Director, Flame Control, commented:

"Flame Control is pleased with LifeSafe's exclusive supply contract for the Netherlands and Benelux. Solutions like MPF and TRF have not been offered in Europe before, and we are proud to present this to the market. This closes the gap in the market for the right products for extinguishing lithium fires which allows us to contribute greatly to fire safety in Europe."

Dominic Berger, Chairman of LifeSafe, commented:

"The distribution agreement with Flame Control is another great milestone for our organisation. After extensive independent testing our product range is proven to extinguish multiple fires including lithium-ion batteries. Our StaySafe All-in-1 extinguisher and new cannisters combined with our Multi-Purpose Fluid offer a one canister option to the market, making it safer and easier for customers to tackle a fire.  Our bulk fluid products are also a new offer in the professional firefighting sector, providing a new range of fluids to safely deal with the increase in dangerous lithium battery fires."

For further enquiries:

LifeSafe Holdings plc

info@lifesafetechnologies.com

Dominic Berger, Chairman

Neil Smith, Chief Executive Officer

Mike Stilwell, Chief Financial Officer

Notes to Editors

LifeSafe is a fire safety technology business that develops eco-friendly, novel and innovative fire extinguishing and prevention fluids and life-saving fire safety products.  LifeSafe has developed a market disrupting range of eco-friendly fire safety protection products; a new patent-pending Thermal Runaway Fluid to combat lithium battery fires by permanently extinguishing and preventing re-ignition, and the StaySafe All-in-1, a handheld eco-friendly and fully recyclable extinguisher which is verified to extinguish ten different types of fire.  LifeSafe is successfully creating new markets for the Group in fire safety through its innovative technologies, products, digital marketing and multi-channel sales; and is continuing to develop new fluid derivations for applications in various industrial market sectors.

LifeSafe was admitted to trading on AIM in July 2022 with the ticker LIFS.

For further information please visit: https://www.lifesafeholdingsplc.com

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/lifesafe-technologies

X (formerly Twitter): https://x.com/LifesafeT

This information is provided by Reach, the non-regulatory press release distribution service of RNS, part of the London Stock Exchange. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

data.title: Oil Retreats Despite Tariff PauseMarket Talk
data.description: Oil prices retreat after a brief rally as U.S. President Trump announced a 90-day pause on so-called reciprocal tariffs for some countries, while also raising duties on China to 125%. Brent crude falls 1.4% to $64.58 a barrel, while WTI is down 1.3% to $61.55 a barrel. The benchmarks settled 4% higher on Wednesday after dropping to their lowest level in more than four years earlier in the session. "While the pause offers some relief to markets, there's still plenty of uncertainty on the trade front," ING analysts say. "This uncertainty is still likely to drag on global growth, which is clearly a concern for oil demand." Meanwhile, the latest EIA figures showed U.S. crude inventories rose for a second consecutive week to their highest level since July. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Euro Could Remain Supported After Trump U-TurnMarket Talk
data.description: The euro should maintain some its recent strength following President Trump's back-and-forth on tariffs, Commerzbank currency analyst Ulrich Leuchtmann says in a note. Trump announced a 90-day pause in higher reciprocal tariffs for most countries but maintained a universal 10% levy. He also raised import duties for China to 125%. While concerns about a U.S. recession could fade, Trump's trade policies could still have long-term negative impact, he says. "The fact that the euro has benefited from the U.S. chaos in recent days may be due to the fact that, for global investors, the only serious alternatives to U.S. Treasuries are German Bunds when safe havens are sought," he says, adding that this strength is likely to remain. The euro rises 0.2% to $1.0973.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Shares End Higher in Anticipation of Stimulus MeasuresMarket Talk
data.description: Chinese shares ended higher in anticipation of stimulus measures to be announced soon. Meanwhile, despite U.S-China trade tensions continuing to escalate, Chinese exporters, who have diversified their operations and supply chains outside of China since President Trump's first term, can still follow the same strategy to bypass tariffs after Trump announced a 90-day tariff pause for some economies, Citi analysts noted. China Tourism Group Duty Free Group rose 5.0% and Wangfujing Group surged 10%. Yonghui Superstores climbed 3.15% and Jason Furniture (Hangzhou) gained 4.9%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 1.2% higher at 3223.64, the Shenzhen Composite Index was up 2.5% and the ChiNext Price Index gained 2.3%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: A producer from China offers Biaxially oriented polypropylene (BOPP) in Myanmar
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Myanmar informed that a Chinese producer is heard to have offered its PP Impact Copolymer grade at the USD 1020/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Myanmar Main port basis.

data.title: OneSource Specialty Unit's Facility in Bengaluru, India Gets Brazilian Regulator's GMP Certification
data.description:
OneSource Specialty Pharma's Unit 2 facility in Bengaluru, India has been granted good manufacturing practices (GMP) certification from Brazilian health regulatory agency ANVISA, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian bourses.

The GMP certification follows a successful regulatory inspection held in November 2024.

The Unit 2 site is dedicated to manufacturing biologics drug substances and finished products, including drug device combinations (DDC) and other injectable products.

With this approval, the company can now supply pharmaceutical products, especially DDCs to the Brazilian market when the customers getting their product approvals.

data.title: Recycled LDPE (R-LDPE) prices witness a rise in Asia
data.description: This week, R-LDPE prices trended upward in the Asia region.A Polymerupdate source in Asia informed a team member,

data.title: A Malaysian producer offers Low density polyethylene (LDPE Coating) in Singapore
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Singapore stated that a producer from Malaysia is heard to have announced its offer for LDPE Coating grades at the USD 1400/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Singapore Main port basis.

data.title: UK's TT Electronics tumbles on FY25 profit warning
data.description:
** TT Electronics tumbles as much as 25.3%; set to record worst day since September 2024

** Co trims FY25 operating profit outlook to 32 mln- 40 mln pounds ($41.13 mln- $51.41 mln) from 40 mln-46 million pounds earlier

** Says will assess all options for components business segment

** CEO Peter France resigns; Eric Lakin named acting CEO effective immediately

** Jefferies says, "Lack of apparent progress in fixing the issues in North America is very concerning"

** TTG down ~22% YTD

($1 = 0.7781 pounds)

data.title: A Singaporean producer offers Polypropylene (PP Block Copolymer) in Malaysia
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Malaysia stated that a producer from Singapore is heard to have announced its offer for PP Block Copolymer grade at the USD 1260/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Malaysia Main port basis.

data.title: European semis on track for best day in over 5 years after tariffs pause
data.description:
** Shares in European semiconductor companies rise after U.S. President Donald Trump paused tariffs for many countries, but hiked China's to 125% from 104%

** STOXX Europe Technology index (.SX8P) is up 9.1%, on track for biggest one-day jump since March 2020, and among the best performers on the wider STOXX 600 which is up 6.9%

** Among single stocks, STMicroelectronics , BESI , ams OSRAM jump between 12% and 13%

** ASML and ASMI are up 9.6%-13.5%, while Aixtron , Siltronic and Infineon rise 9.4%, 13% and 15.1%, respectively

** Nordic Semiconductor and Soitec are up 12% and 14.5%, respectively

data.title: Enact Holdings to Replace SolarWinds in S&P SmallCap 600
data.description:
Enact Holdings will replace SolarWinds in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective before the opening of trading on April 16, S&P Dow Jones Indices said late Wednesday.

Turn/River Capital is set to acquire SolarWinds in a deal expected to close soon, the market research and indexing firm said.

data.title: China's Consumer Price Data Shows Economy Still Not out of the WoodsMarket Talk
data.description: China's weaker-than-expected inflation data shows that its economy is still not out of the woods, according to Barclays analysts in a research note. China's headline CPI deflation extended for a second month in March despite low base effects, reflecting demand indicators still lag supply indicators, the analysts say. "We see rising risks of persistent low inflation in the coming years, in view of severe and widespread excess capacity and structural weakness in consumption and property investment, which collectively account for about 70% of GDP," they add. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)
